text:
Novak Djokoviccleared a major hurdle is his drive for Olympic gold when he beat Rafael Nadal in a lopsided second-round match inParis.

The Serbian was in irresistible form with a 6-1, 6-4, victory proving to be a stunning defeat for the 14 timesFrench Openchampion, who had won 113 of his 116 previous matches on the Roland Garros clay.

Djokovic is the top seed and should stroll into the final despite having a potential meeting with Stefanos Tsitsipas further down the line. He is 8/11 with Betway and bet365 to reach the showpiece and a widespread 2/1 to win it, although our money is still on ante-post selectionCarlos Alcaraz.

It would be a major shock if Djokovic lost to his next opponent, Dominik Koepfer, who is ranked 64th in the world and whose best effort at the French Open is reaching the third round three years ago.

However, the German could take a set off a complacent Djokovic and, although we could have egg on our faces after another one-sided affair, it’s worth having a minor interest in Koepfer winning either the first or second sets which are both priced at 6/1 with Betway.

Talk is cheap when figuring out the Sussex Stakes, the feature race on day two of Glorious Goodwood, but take notice of Charlie Appleby’s Notable Speech.

Many will expect a repeat of the thrilling tussle at Royal Ascot which saw Richard Hannon’s Rosallion win the St James’ Palace Stakes, getting up on the line under Sean Levey to pip the Aidan O’Brien-trained Henry Longfellow by a neck.

The bookmakers expect Rosallion to triumph again at no better than evens with William Hill while Henry Longfellow is 4-1 with Ladbrokes, Coral, Bet Victor and BoyleSports.

Notable Speech was never in contention in the St James’ and finished a disappointing seventh of eight, suffering his first defeat in an otherwise impressive career which includes a splendid triumph in the 2000 Guineas at Newmarket in May.

That form has held up remarkably well with Rosallion beaten by one-and-a-half lengths in second while Haatem in third swept to victory in the Jersey Stakes at Royal Ascot where fourth-placed Inershin won the Commonwealth Cup.

And don’t forget City of Troy, way down the field in the 2000 Guineas but majestically improving to win the Derby at Epsom and the Coral-Eclipse at Sandown.

Appleby is a tremendous trainer and with the wonderful William Buick in the saddle they can rectify Notable Speech’s poor performance last time out and return to the winner’s enclosure at 9/2 with Ladbrokes and Coral.

Aesterius and Celandine have pace to burn in the Molecomb but Soldier’s Heart has the benefit of running at Goodwood before, winning by nearly four lengths at the end of June, and is 10/1 with William Hill, Betfred, Paddy Power and bet365 among others.

Ralph Beckett’s Stop The Cavalry defied a top weight of 9st 11lb when winning a useful handicap at Chester and a drop to 8st 13lb should see her go well in the Oak Tree Stakes. She’s interesting at 11/1 with Betfred, Betfair, Hills and Paddy Power.

Day two begins with the Shaker and it’s difficult not to fall for the Gosden yard’s Lord of Love, who has won his last two outings and is 11/2 with Ladbrokes and Coral.

Spain, our ante-post tip to win women’s football gold at the Olympics, are 7/10 with Unibet and BetMGM to beat Brazil in what should be a hugely entertaining affair.

title: Novak Djokovic can sweep past Dominik Koepfer in Olympic gold chase | Metro News
description: Novak Djokovic is in great shape as he aims to better the Olympic bronze he won in Beijing in 2008.
